title: Helixanthera sampsonii
taxonomy: Helixanthera sampsonii is classified within the family Loranthaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as mistletoes. This plant is native to China and is a type of hemiparasitic plant, meaning it obtains some of its nutrients by connecting to the roots of other plants. The Loranthaceae family is a part of the order Santalales, which consists of various types of parasitic and hemiparasitic plants. Helixanthera sampsonii is a species within the genus Helixanthera, which contains several other species of mistletoes. The classification of Helixanthera sampsonii reflects its unique characteristics and relationships with other plants.
